name: buzz
description: Buzz transcribes and translates audio offline on your personal computer. Powered by OpenAI's Whisper.
readme_summary: Buzz is a desktop application that transcribes and translates audio and video files offline on your personal computer, powered by OpenAI's Whisper. It offers advanced features like live transcription, speech separation, speaker identification, and multiple backend support for GPU acceleration.
key_features: Transcribe audio and video files or Youtube links
Live realtime audio transcription from microphone
Multiple whisper backend support (CUDA, Apple Silicon, Vulkan)
Export transcripts to TXT, SRT, and VTT
primary_use_case: Offline audio and video transcription and translation on a personal computer.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, anthropomorphic robot with a large ear, sitting at a desk in a cozy home office. The robot is holding a microphone and a tablet displaying a waveform. From the microphone, sound waves are visibly transforming into clear, neatly organized text bubbles that float towards the tablet. The robot is also holding a small globe, with arrows indicating translation. Subtle digital particles and a small, stylized 'Buzz' logo on the robot's chest connect it to technology. The overall mood is helpful and efficient, with warm, inviting lighting. The style should be a charming 3D isometric illustration with clean lines and a soft color palette.

name: wayback-machine-webextension
description: A web browser extension for Chrome, Firefox, Edge, and Safari 14.
readme_summary: This web browser extension provides direct access to the Internet Archive's Wayback Machine, allowing users to view historical versions of web pages, save current pages, and receive contextual information. It aims to preserve web history and provide users with tools to explore and understand how URLs have evolved over time.
key_features: View Oldest, Newest & Overview of archived pages
404 Not Found error handling with archive check
Relevant Resources (Wikipedia, Amazon Books, TV News)
primary_use_case: To allow web browser users to easily access, view, and contribute to the historical archive of web pages maintained by the Internet Archive's Wayback Machine.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical, antique-looking time machine console, glowing with soft blue light, sits on a desk. A user's hand is reaching out to a large, ornate dial labeled 'Web History'. As the dial turns, a holographic projection above it displays a timeline of a website's evolution, showing different visual snapshots of its appearance over the years. Small, subtle UI elements resembling browser extension pop-ups are integrated into the console's design. The background is a softly blurred library or study, suggesting knowledge and history. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a warm, inviting color palette, emphasizing the ease of accessing and understanding web history.

name: srt-transmitter
description: SRT Stream Manager is a desktop application for managing multiple SRT streams. It allows you to start, stop, and monitor streams with a beautiful interface. Built with Electron and React.
readme_summary: SRT Stream Manager is a cross-platform desktop application built with Electron, React, and TypeScript for managing multiple SRT streams. It offers a user-friendly interface to start, stop, monitor, and configure streams, with features like bulk operations, real-time logs, and persistent storage.
primary_use_case: Managing and monitoring multiple SRT (Secure Reliable Transport) streams through a desktop graphical user interface.
cover_image_prompt: A conductor standing in front of a grand orchestra, with each musician representing an individual SRT stream. The conductor, holding a glowing baton, gestures to start, stop, or adjust the tempo (stream status) of different sections of the orchestra. Sheet music on the podium displays stream configurations and real-time data feeds. Subtle digital particles flow from the baton, visualizing data transmission. The background is a clean, modern control room with screens showing stream health. The repository name 'SRT Stream Manager' is subtly integrated into a console display. The style is a 3D isometric illustration with vibrant colors and clear, defined elements, emphasizing control and orchestration.

name: fasthttp
description: ⚡ Async HTTP client with expressive logging and route-based requests
readme_summary: Fasthttp is a Python library that provides an asynchronous HTTP client with features like expressive logging, route-based requests, and support for HTTP/2. It simplifies making HTTP requests with a clean API, decorators, and middleware for request/response interception.
key_features: Access to request details from response object
primary_use_case: Making asynchronous HTTP requests in Python with enhanced logging, type safety, and middleware capabilities.
cover_image_prompt: A highly efficient, stylized mail carrier sorting and delivering a multitude of colorful envelopes at lightning speed. Each envelope represents an HTTP request, and the carrier is depicted with glowing, digital trails, symbolizing asynchronous processing. Some envelopes are being scanned by a small, unobtrusive scanner that reveals their contents (data) and destination (URL) in a clear, readable format. The background is a clean, modern cityscape with subtle code snippets integrated into the architecture. The overall mood is one of speed, efficiency, and clarity, with a vibrant color palette. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration, emphasizing the flow and organization of requests and responses.

name: tray-rain
readme_summary: Tray Rain is a small, amateur Windows desktop application that displays the current temperature and the likelihood of rain for the day directly from the system tray. It allows users to select their location using a map-based picker and configures settings like timezone and update intervals via a JSON file.
key_features: Displays current temperature in system tray
Indicates expected rain for the day
Configurable settings (timezone, refresh interval, rain threshold)
primary_use_case: To provide users with a quick, at-a-glance view of local weather conditions, specifically temperature and the probability of rain, directly from their Windows system tray without needing to open a full application.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, stylized cloud character peeking out from a Windows system tray icon. The cloud is gently raining droplets, and a small, clear thermometer icon is integrated into its design, showing a warm temperature. Beside the cloud, a subtle, glowing map pin icon indicates a chosen location. In the background, a faint, abstract visualization of weather data flows like digital rain. The overall mood is helpful and unobtrusive, with a clean, modern illustration style. The repository name 'Tray Rain' is subtly incorporated into the tray bar design. The scene should be bright and inviting, emphasizing ease of use and immediate information.
